arts.ttl: Inside the magic of Plymouth’s FA Cup run - and its deeper meaning
arts.sum: As the Championship side travel to Manchester City in the fifth round, captain Joe Edwards tells Miguel Delaney about the club’s wider role and commitment to environmentally conscious choices

arts.ttl: Plymouth’s Maksym Talovierov: ‘When the fans cheer, it just goes inside of me’
arts.sum: Ukraine defender on his parents enduring the war, the generosity of fans and facing Manchester City in the FA Cup Suddenly talking about celebrating tackles with gladiatorial grace and the prospect of swapping shirts with Erling Haaland feels rather insignificant. Maksym Talovierov, known as Maxi, is detailing the sobering reality of life since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine. “It’s really hard because for three years every morning I wake up and I see a lot of notifications about rockets and missiles in every city, including Kyiv, where my parents are,” the Plymouth defender says. “I text them immediately to see how they’re doing. ‘Are you in the [underground] car park? Are you in the metro?’ Because when they [Russia] are bombing and it’s dangerous, that’s where they go because they are the closest things to safe zones.” The 24-year-old has not seen his mother, Maryna, and father, Vadym, a former professional footballer, for three and a half years. “Sometimes they might not have a connection or wifi and then it’s stressful because they don’t respond and you don’t know what’s going on. On the news it might say: ‘In this area of Kyiv, five rockets.’ But you never know exactly where. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Man City's Rodri returns to individual training
arts.sum: Rodri is back in training at Manchester City, completing an individual workout at the club's Etihad Campus on Friday.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid fined by UEFA and threatened with future stadium ban following homophobic chants aimed at Man City boss Pep Guardiola
arts.sum: In addition to the club's fine, Madrid have been warned by European football's governing body that further bad behaviour could result in a partial stadium ban.

arts.ttl: Is there VAR and extra time in FA Cup fifth round? Rules explained for Man United and Man City
arts.sum: Manchester United and Manchester City are in FA Cup fifth round action this weekend as they aim to reach the quarter-final of the competition
What makes an FA Cup giant-killer? BBC Sport looks at some of Plymouth's heroes in the competition so far.

arts.ttl: Man City's Pep Guardiola delivers blunt response to Javier Tebas accusations, La Liga legal complain
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola delivers a blunt response to accusations aimed at Man City from La Liga president Javier Tebas, who confirms that a legal complaint has been filed to the EU Commission against the Citizens.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich chief confirms they will try to sign Bayer Leverkusen star Florian Wirtz this summer - with Bundesliga giants set to battle Man City for 'the best player in Germany'
arts.sum: Bayern Munich chief Karl-Heinz Rummenigge has announced that his club will target the signing of Bayer Leverkusen's Florian Wirtz this summer.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola has his say on De Bruyne’s Man City future
arts.sum: Incredibly, Manchester City’s FA Cup game against Plymouth Argyle on Saturday represents their final chance of picking up silverware this season. There has been no movement on a contract extension for De Bruyne and, with injury issues becoming increasingly prominent, the Belgian great looks set to leave the club in the near future.

arts.ttl: Man City vs. Plymouth: "Giant" Pep Guardiola labelled "one of the most successful coaches in sports history" by Miron Muslic
arts.sum: Man City manager Pep Guardiola is described as "one of the most successful coaches in sports history" by Plymouth Argyle boss Miron Muslic ahead of Saturday's FA Cup fifth-round tie.
